properties.remark: EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES SOUTH OF THE ALEXANDRIA AIRPORT. A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN IN A FIELD SOUTH OF HIGHWAY 28 BEFORE THE TORNADO DAMAGED BUILDINGS ON ROSS LANE. ONE HOUSE LOST PART OF ITS ROOF AND DEBRIS FROM THE HOUSE WAS THROWN UP TO 100 YARDS. A CARPORT COLLPASED ON A VEHICLE AND POWERLINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. A HIGHWAY DEPARTMENT WAREHOUSE BUILDING HAD ONE GARAGE DOOR BLOWN INWARD AND DESTROYED. PATH LENGTH OF 1/4 MILE AND PATH WIDTH OF 20 YARDS.
911 RAPIDES PARISH REPORTING A TORNADO NEAR ROSS LANE AND HWY 28 WEST. DAMAGE TO A HOUSE WITH ROOF BLOWN OFF ON TO CAR AND POWERLINES DOWN. JUST SOUTH OF THE AEX AIRPORT.

A LARGE DOWNBURST BEGAN ABOUT 1 MILE S OF PINE PRAIRIE AND ENDED ABOUT 7 MILES NE OF VILLE PLATTE. THIS AREA OF WIND DAMAGE WAS AROUND 15 MILES LONG AND UP TO 3 MILES WIDE. WITHIN THIS ZONE...SCATTERED TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN FROM NW TO SE WITH SEVERAL ROADS BLOCKED BY FALLEN TREES. POWER LINES WERE ALSO BLOWN DOWN WITH A FEW OUTBUILDINGS RECEIVING DAMAGE. NO EVIDENCE OF A TORNADO WAS FOUND.

TREES DOWN...POWER LINES DOWN...ABOVE GROUND POOLS FULL OF WATER FLIP OVER AND DAMAGING FENCES. OVER 1000 CALLS OF POWER OUTAGES TO LOCAL ELECTRIC CO. DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG OLD PARK ROAD BETWEEN US 167 AND LA 3042 JUST WEST OF CHICOT STATE PARK.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND SIGNIFCANT WIND DAMAGE FROM A MICROBURST ACROSS DEQUINCY. ONE DOWNTOWN BUILDING HAD ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF AND A TRAILER ON FOURTH STREET WAS DAMAGED. SEVERAL OTHER BUILDINGS RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS LARGE TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. ALL DAMAGE WAS IN A NNW TO SSE DIRECTION WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 70 MPH.
TREES DOWN IN DERIDDER ON HARVEY CROSSING ROAD REPORTED BY KPLC-TV.
NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE ALONG HIGHWAY 12 WEST OF THE KERNAN COMMUNITY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN FROM NW TO SE.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE IN THE BUHLER COMMUNITY ALONG HIGHWAY 27 AND BANKENS RD. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND A RADIO TOWER AT A GAS PLANT WAS TOPPLED. SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES HAD MINOR DAMAGE TO SKIRTING AND SIDING. ALL DAMAGE WAS IN A NW TO SE DIRECTION WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 70 MPH.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS THUNDERSTORM MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE WITHIN SUBDIVISIONS ALONG AND SOUTH OF CAL CAM LINE ROAD. EXTENT OF DAMAGE INCLUDED METAL STORAGE BUILDINGS ROLLED OVER AND DESTROYED...METAL CARPORT RIPPED OFF HOME WITH PIECES LANDING ON BOAT AND REMAINDER OF YARD. NUMEROUS MOBILE HOMES HAS SKIRTING RIPPED OFF. ALL DEBRIS WAS BLOWN FROM WEST TO EAST DIRECTION.
A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN IN THE SOUTHERN SECTIONS OF LAKE CHARLES.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE IN THE HAMPTON COMMUNITY ALONG COTTONGIN CASTOR ROAD AT HIGHWAY 104. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN WITH ONE FALLING ON A HOUSE. A MOBILE HOME HAD ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF. ALL DAMAGE WAS IN A WNW TO ESE DIRECTION WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 70 MPH.
REPORTED BY WEATHER UNDERGROUND GAGE AT BIG LAKE.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS THUNDERSTORM MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE WITHIN SUBDIVISIONS ALONG AND SOUTH OF CAL CAM LINE ROAD. EXTENT OF DAMAGE INCLUDED METAL STORAGE BUILDINGS ROLLED OVER AND DESTROYED...METAL CARPORT RIPPED OFF HOME WITH PIECES LANDING ON BOAT AND REMAINDER OF YARD. NUMEROUS MOBILE HOMES HAS SKIRTING RIPPED OFF. ALL DEBRIS WAS BLOWN FROM WEST TO EAST DIRECTION.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE ALONG A 3 MILE LONG PATH FROM 3 WSW MAMOU TO 2 SSW MAMOU. ALONG HIGHWAY 104 NEAR LD VERETTE RD SEVERAL HOMES RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. ALONG HIGHWAY 13 SOUTH OF MAMOU SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED OR BLOWN DOWN. ALL DAMAGE WAS IN A NW TO SE DIRECTION WITH ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 70 MPH.
WIDESPREAD WIND DAMAGE IN MAMOU WITH NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AND POWER OUT TO ENTIRE TOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO. REPORTED BY KATC-TV. TIME BASED ON RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE ALONG PRAIRIE RONDE HIGHWAY BETWEEN CHANTEBERRY ROAD AND RAPHAEL ROAD. A CONVENIENCE STORE AT THE INTERSECTION WITH FONTENOT ROAD HAD ITS ROOF PARTIALLY RIPPED OFF WITH DEBRIS BLOWN ACROSS PRAIRIE RONDE HIGHWAY INTO A RESIDENCE. SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE HIGHWAY. ALL DAMAGE WAS IN A WEST TO EAST DIRECTION WITH ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 65 MPH.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND INTENSE MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE NORTH OF LAWTELL ALONG BERTRAND RD. ONE TIED DOWN MOBILE HOME WAS FLIPPED OVER ONTO SEVERAL VEHICLES AND DESTROYED. DEBRIS FROM THE MOBILE HOME WAS BLOWN UP TO 200 YARDS AWAY ACROSS A FIELD. A NEARBY BARN SUFFERED MAJOR ROOF DAMAGE. OTHER MOBILE HOMES IN THE AREA RECEIVED MINOR TO MODERATE DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. ALL DAMAGE WAS IN A WSW TO ENE DIRECTION WITH ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 90 MPH.
TRAILER FLIPPED OVER ON BERTRAND ROAD IN LAWTELL REPORTED BY ST LANDRY PARISH SHERIFF OFFICE.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND MICROBURST WIND DAMAGE ON THE NORTH END OF MILA ROAD NEAR THE KINGS TRUCK STOP ON HIGHWAY 190. ONE MOBILE HOME ON CINDER BLOCKS WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND ANOTHER HAD PART OF ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF. DEBRIS FROM BOTH MOBILE HOMES BLEW INTO A THIRD MOBILE HOME CAUSING SEVERE DAMAGE TO ITS SIDING AND ROOF. TWO OUTBUILDINGS WERE ALSO DAMAGED. ALL DEBRIS WAS BLOWN FROM SW TO NE WITH ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 90 MPH.
ONE MOBILE HOME WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED BY MICROBURST WINDS AND SEVERAL OTHERS WERE DAMAGED ON MILA ROAD NEAR THE KINGS TRUCK STOP ON HIGHWAY 190.

KATC-TV IS REPORTING SEVERAL HOMES DAMAGED AND ONE INJURY POSSIBLE NEAR CATAHOULA.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF1 TORNADO STARTED IN A FEILD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF LD DRIVE AND HIGGINBOTHAM RD WITH LARGE TREE BRANCHES DOWN AND DEBRIS IN TREES. TORNADO CONTINUED ENE ACROSS ACROSS THE SUBDIVISION WHERE IT DAMAGED ROOFS OF HOUSES AND MOBILE HOMES. A MOBILE HOME FLIPPED AT LEAST A COUPLE TIMES COMPLETELY DESTROYING THE MOBILE HOME. A CAMPER TRAILER COMPLETELY FLIPPED OVER ON A CAR. THE TORNADO EXITED THE SUBDIVISION AT ESTATES AVE INTO OPEN FIELD. TORNADO WAS HALF MILE LONG AND 50 YARDS WIDE.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF1 TORNADO STARTED IN A FEILD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF LD DRIVE AND HIGGINBOTHAM RD WITH LARGE TREE BRANCHES DOWN AND DEBRIS IN TREES. TORNADO CONTINUED ENE ACROSS ACROSS THE SUBDIVISION WHERE IT DAMAGED ROOFS OF HOUSES AND MOBILE HOMES. A MOBILE HOME FLIPPED AT LEAST A COUPLE TIMES COMPLETELY DESTROYING THE MOBILE HOME. A CAMPER TRAILER COMPLETELY FLIPPED OVER ON A CAR. THE TORNADO EXITED THE SUBDIVISION AT ESTATES AVE INTO AN OPEN FIELD. TORNADO WAS HALF MILE LONG AND 50 YARDS WIDE.

TROPICAL FUNNEL CLOUD BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN AT FIRESTONE PLANT ON HWY 108. OBSERVER SAW FUNNEL CLOUD TOUCH DOWN IN FRONT OF HIM AND BLEW SMALL DEBRIS AROUND. RADAR ONLY SHOWED A SMALL SHOWER AT THAT TIME.

KATC-TV REPORTED WIND DAMAGE ALONG OLIAS ROAD SOUTHEAST OF ERATH. THE ROOF OF ONE TRAILER HOME WAS PARTIALLY PEELED OFF AND THE ADJACENT CARPORT WAS BLOWN DOWN. DEBRIS FROM THE HOME WAS WRAPPED AROUND A TELEPHONE POLE AND BOARDS IMPLANTED IN THE GROUND. TWO SHEDS AT NEARBY HOMES WERE DAMAGED AND TURNED OVER.

ADDITIONAL STORM SURVEYS FOUND AN EF0 TORNADO BEGAN FURTHER SOUTH THAN ORIGINALLY INDICATED. THIS TORNADO FORMED SOUTH OF POM ROY ROAD THEN MOVED NORTHWARD CROSSING HWY 14 BEFORE ENDING 5 MILES SSE OF ROANOKE. NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS AND A FEW TREES WERE DOWNED ALONG THE PATH. SEVERAL POWER LINES AND A FEW POWER POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON HWY 380 AND FURTHER NORTH ON LYONS ROAD. NUMEROUS EYEWITNESSES WATCHED AND PHOTOGRAPHED THE TORNADO. TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 8 MILES AND A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS.

EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTH OF HIGHWAY 14 AND MOVED NORTHWARD THROUGH RURAL AREAS BEFORE ENDING 5 MILES SSE OF ROANOKE. SEVERAL POWER LINES AND POWER POLES WERE DOWNED ALONG HIGHWAY 380 AND LYONS ROAD. NUMEROUS EYEWITNESSES WATCHED AND PHOTOGRAPHED THE TORNADO. TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 5 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS.

*** 1 INJ *** A LARGE MULTIVORTEX TORNADO BEGAN SOUTH OF HWY 90 WEST OF JENNINGS AND MOVED NORTHWARD BEFORE ENDING ALONG HWY 395 SOUTHWEST OF ELTON. THE TORNADO WAS UP TO ONE HALF MILE WIDE EARLY IN THE PATH BEFORE NARROWING NORTH OF I-10. A TRACTOR TRAILER RIG WAS BLOWN INTO THE MEDIAN OF I-10 INJURING THE DRIVER. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALL ALONG THE PATH WITH SEVERAL HOMES AND OUTBUILDINGS RECEIVING MINOR DAMAGE. TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 16 MILES WITH A MAX PATH WIDTH OF 1/2 MILE.

ANOTHER LARGE HIGH END EF0 TORNADO BEGAN SOUTH OF DAN BULLER ROAD AND MOVED NORTHWARD THROUGH ELTON. TREES AND A BARN WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED ON DAN BULLER ROAD. AS THE TORNADO MOVED ACROSS ELTON NUMEROUS LARGE TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN WITH DAMAGE TO 15 HOMES. THE WORST DAMAGE OCCURRED NORTH OF HWY 190 BETWEEN WASHINGTON STREET AND LANGLEY ROAD. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED NORTH OF ELTON ALONG BULLER ROAD. TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 5 MILES WITH A MAX PATH WIDTH OF 1/2 MILE.

AN EF0 TORNADO BEGAN ABOUT 1 MILE EAST OF THE PRUDHOMME COMMUNITY AND MOVED NNE INTO ST LANDRY PARISH BEFORE ENDING JUST NORTH OF HWY 190 ABOUT 1 MILE EAST OF SAVOY. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG HWY 358 AND WAYNE RD. ONE BARN WAS DAMAGED ON WAYNE RD AND DEBRIS FROM THE BARN WAS CARRIED ACROSS HWY 190. A RESIDENT ON WAYNE RD WATCHED THE TORNADO CROSS HIS CRAWFISH POND ACROSS THE STREET. PATH LENGTH OF 3 MILES AND PATH WIDTH OF 25 YARDS.

TORNADO BEGAN ABOUT 4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF GUEYDAN THEN MOVED NORTHEAST TO THE SOUTH OF GUEYDAN WHERE THE MOST EXTENSIVE DAMAGE OCCURRED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED THROUGH GUEYDAN AND EXITED THE TOWN NORTH OF THE WATER TOWER WITH THE LAST DAMAGE NOTED ABOUT 2 MILES NORTHEAST OF GUEYDAN. IN ALL ABOUT 50 STRUCTURES RECEIVED DAMAGE WITH 2 COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND 7 WITH MODERATE TO MAJOR DAMAGE. PATH LENGTH OF 6 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH FROM 40 TO 70 YARDS WIDE. AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE OLD RACETRACK 1 MILE NORTHEAST OF SAVOY AND MOVED NORTHWARD CROSSING INTO SOUTHEASTERN EVANGELINE PARISH BEFORE DISSIPATING 2 MILES SOUTHEAST OF CHATAIGNIER. AN OUTBUILDING WAS DAMAGED SOUTH OF LINZAY ROAD WITH DEBRIS CARRIED ACROSS FIELDS TO THE NORTHEAST. SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN OR UPROOTED NORTH OF LINZAY ROAD AND ALONG HIGHWAY 95. PRELIM REPORT FROM NWS SURVEY CONFIRMS TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE IN GUEYDAN. 2 HOMES COMPLETELY DESTROYED...SEVERAL OTHER HOMES DAMAGED. TORNADO BEGAN 1 MILE SOUTH OF GUEYDAN AND LIFTED JUST NORTH OF GUEYDAN. EARLIER EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED PERSON TRAPPED IN DAMAGED BUILDING. PERSON WAS OKAY AND NO OTHER INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

TORNADO BEGAN ABOUT 4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF GUEYDAN. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED NORTHEAST TO THE SOUTH OF GUEYDAN WHERE THE MOST EXTENSIVE DAMAGE OCCURRED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ON INTO GUEYDAN AND EXITED GUEYDAN NORTH OF THE WATER TOWER. THE LAST DAMAGE NOTED WAS ABOUT 1 MILE NORTH OF GUEYDAN. IN ALL ABOUT 50 STRUCTURES RECEIVED DAMAGE WITH 2 COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND 7 WITH MODERATE TO MAJOR DAMAGE. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 4.7 MILES LONG. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 40 TO 70 YARDS WIDE.

A SECOND TORNADO FORMED FROM THE SAME SUPERCELL THUNDERSTORM THAT PRODUCED THE GUEYDAN TORNADO.
